description:
GOAL: Make `==` and `!=` perform structural (deep) equality for heap-typed values, not pointer-bit equality. Today every BinOp::Eq in ir_codegen is `icmp eq, av, bvv` which is correct for ints / atoms / specials / nil but wrong for two distinct-but-same-content lists / tuples / bitstrings / maps.

## Why this is a separate ticket

Spotted while landing `.11.13` (Maps). Maps were the immediate motivation, but the dispatch shape is identical for lists, tuples, bitstrings, and maps — better to rewrite `BinOp::Eq` once than to touch it four times. This ticket centralizes that rewrite.

## Decisions captured

- Bit-equality stays the fast path. At every `BinOp::Eq` / `Neq`, codegen emits a tag check on both operands; only when both are Tag::Ptr does it call into a runtime helper. Atoms compare by id-bits, ints by int-bits, specials by bit pattern — already correct under bit-equality.
- Two heap values of *different* kinds compare unequal regardless of contents (e.g., `[1, 2] != {1, 2}`).
- Cycles aren't reachable in fz value-land (no mutation; lists/tuples/maps/bitstrings are persistent), so the recursive deep-eq doesn't need cycle detection. If user-records arrive that allow self-reference, revisit.
- No structural ordering (`<` / `>`) over heap values in this ticket — only `==` / `!=`. Ordered comparisons remain int-only at the JIT level.

## Deliverables

src/ir_codegen.rs:
- New runtime fn `fz_struct_eq(a_bits: u64, b_bits: u64) -> u32` returning 0/1. Dispatches on shared HeapKind:
  * List: walk both cons chains, deep-compare head + tail at each step
  * Struct (tuples): compare schema_id; if equal, compare each FzValue field via fz_struct_eq
  * Bitstring: compare bit_len then bytes (full bytes + final partial byte under bit-mask)
  * Map: walk sorted entries pairwise; equal iff every key+value pair deep-eq
  * Different kinds, or same-kind-but-shape-mismatch: return 0
- `BinOp::Eq` / `Neq` codegen path: emit a tag-check fast path. If both operands' low 3 bits == 0 (Tag::Ptr), call `fz_struct_eq`; else fall back to `icmp eq`. Cranelift can express this with a select-based blend or an explicit branch + phi.
- Rewire the affected codegen lines (currently in `lower_prim` under `BinOp::Eq` / `Neq` arms).

## Tests

- `[1, 2, 3] == [1, 2, 3]` returns true (distinct allocations, same content).
- `[1, 2] == [1, 2, 3]` returns false (length mismatch).
- `{1, :ok} == {1, :ok}` returns true.
- `{1, 2} == {1, 2, 3}` returns false (different schema_id).
- `<<1, 2, 3>> == <<1, 2, 3>>` returns true; same with sub-byte bit_len.
- `%{a: 1, b: 2} == %{b: 2, a: 1}` returns true (sorted layout makes order irrelevant).
- `%{a: 1, b: 2} == %{a: 1, b: 3}` returns false.
- Heterogeneous: `[1, 2] == {1, 2}` returns false.
- Nested: `%{x: [1, 2]} == %{x: [1, 2]}` returns true.
- Existing scalar-Eq tests (atom_const, mutual_recursion's `Bool(true)` halt, etc.) still pass byte-for-byte.

## Out of scope

- Ordered comparisons (`<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=`) on heap values.
- Structural equality with user-defined `==/2` overrides (no overload mechanism in fz today).
- Cycle detection (not reachable under current value-land semantics).
- Float deep-eq via `==` (covered separately by fz-ul4.11.20 — that ticket already lists "two distinct boxed floats with same value compare equal" as a goal; this ticket and that one will both touch the BinOp::Eq path, so whichever lands second integrates cleanly).

## Cross-references

- Deferred from fz-ul4.11.13 landing (2026-05-10). The map-equality test in that ticket's deliverables is satisfied by tests here.
- Touches the same BinOp::Eq codegen as fz-ul4.11.20 (boxed floats). No hard ordering — whichever lands first picks up half the dispatch logic, and the second ticket extends it.

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: none — all heap kinds it touches (List, Struct, Bitstring, Map) are landed.
- BLOCKS: none currently filed.

comments.text:
Implementation notes:

- Both-ptr inline test replaces the .11.20 either-ptr dispatch: '(a|b) & 7 == 0' is a 3-op fast path that fires only when there's actually a heap value to inspect. Cross-tag pairs (Ptr vs Int, etc.) take the inline icmp eq path with the correct false answer.
- fz_value_eq is now a thin shim around eq_fz, an internal recursive equality. eq_fz short-circuits on raw bit-eq (handles all scalar same-tag and ptr-identity cases), refuses cross-tag, then dispatches per HeapKind:
  - Float: read payload, value-compare (.11.20 contract preserved)
  - List: walk both cons chains in lockstep, eq_fz head, recurse on tail
  - Struct: schema_id must match; iterate fields per schema, eq_fz each
  - Bitstring: bit_len match, full bytes equal, last partial byte masked
  - Map: count match, walk canonical-sorted entries pairwise
  - Closure / Vec / future kinds: ptr-identity (already false at that point); explicit per-ticket scope
- Different kinds compare unequal via the kind-mismatch short-circuit.
- 10 new ir_codegen tests: list (same-content distinct, length mismatch), tuple (same arity content, different arity), bitstring (byte-aligned), map (sort-order-independent, value mismatch), heterogeneous List vs Tuple, nested Map<Atom, List>, Neq inverts.

title: Structural equality for heap-typed values
